name: Sierra de Tejeda – Circular from Puente
name_original: Sierra de Tejeda – circular desde Puente de Don Manuel
description: Tackle the difficult 22.2-mile mountain bike loop, Sierra de Tejeda – circular desde Puente de Don Manuel, with 4055 feet of climbing.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Alcaucín offers diverse landscapes within the Sierra Tejeda Mountains, providing challenging terrain for outdoor enthusiasts. The region features ancient pine forests, olive groves, and avocado and mango plantations, which create varied and scenic routes. Riders can explore the foothills and higher elevations, encountering a rich variety of flora and fauna. This area is characterized by its natural features, including the dramatic backdrop of the mountains and extensive trail networks. Best mountain bike trails around Alcaucín The most popular mountain bike trail is Sierra de Tejeda – Circular from Puente, a difficult 22.2 miles (35.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 48 minutes to complete, offering significant climbing through the Sierra Tejeda range. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Miguel Álvarez Viewpoint – Alcaucín Village loop from Alcaucín, a difficult 18.9 miles (30.5 km) path. This route provides extensive views and varied terrain around the village. Local mountain bikers also love the Miguel Álvarez Viewpoint – El Aguadero Bridge loop from Taj Majal, a 25.9 miles (41.6 km) trail leading through diverse landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 48 minutes. Mountain biking around Alcaucín is defined by the Sierra Tejeda Mountains, ancient pine forests, and varied terrain including olive and avocado groves. The network offers options for different ability levels, with more than 25 routes ranging from easy to difficult. The routes in Alcaucín are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Alcaucín's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Alcaucín?
A: There are over 25 mountain bike trails around Alcaucín, offering a variety of experiences. The komoot community has documented 29 routes in total, ranging from easy to difficult.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Alcaucín?
A: Yes, while many routes are moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy mountain bike trail available. These trails often pass through scenic areas like olive groves and pine forests, providing a gentler introduction to the region's diverse terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Alcaucín?
A: The terrain around Alcaucín is highly varied, situated within the Sierra Tejeda Mountains. You'll encounter ancient pine forests, olive groves, and avocado and mango plantations. Trails can range from challenging mountain climbs with significant elevation changes to more rolling paths through agricultural areas, offering diverse landscapes and surfaces.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Alcaucín?
A: Mountain biking in Alcaucín offers stunning views and access to unique natural features. You can explore areas with panoramic vistas of the Sierra Tejeda Mountains, pass through the historic Boquete de Zafarraya mountain pass, or even catch glimpses of Lake Viñuela. Notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/448453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Maroma Summit&lt;/a&gt; (the highest peak in the range) and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1116374" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zafarraya Plain&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also offer views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3895596" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Saltillo Suspension Bridge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Alcaucín?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Alcaucín are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example of a challenging circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10301799" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miguel Álvarez Viewpoint – Alcaucín Village loop from Alcaucín&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 30.5 km with significant climbing.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Alcaucín?
A: Alcaucín generally offers good conditions for mountain biking for much of the year. The region's Mediterranean climate means mild winters and hot summers. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with pleasant temperatures and lush landscapes. Even in winter, many trails remain accessible, though higher elevations might be cooler.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Alcaucín?
A: The mountain bike trails around Alcaucín are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.0 stars from over 40 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, the challenging climbs, and the extensive views of the Sierra Tejeda Mountains and surrounding white villages.

Q: Are there any specific routes that offer a good challenge for experienced mountain bikers?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994115133/sierra-de-tejeda-circular-from-puente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Tejeda – Circular from Puente&lt;/a&gt; are excellent choices. This difficult 35.8 km trail involves substantial climbing through the Sierra Tejeda range, providing a demanding and rewarding experience.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay near the mountain bike trails in Alcaucín?
A: Alcaucín and its surrounding villages offer various options for refreshments and accommodation. The region is geared towards active tourism, with local establishments providing services for visitors. You'll find cafes and restaurants in the village centers, and various types of accommodation ranging from rural guesthouses to hotels.

Q: Are there any other outdoor activities besides mountain biking in Alcaucín?
A: Yes, Alcaucín is a hub for outdoor activities. Beyond mountain biking, the region is popular for hiking, with trails like the 'Ruta del Boquete de Zafarraya' and 'Sendero de La Maroma'. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5061410" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baños de Vilo Hot Springs&lt;/a&gt; or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3895596" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Saltillo Suspension Bridge&lt;/a&gt; for another adventurous experience.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The El Alcázar Natural Park area, located above Alcaucín, is particularly known for its extraordinary nature. Sightings of Iberian goats and ibex are common, especially in the higher, more rugged parts of the Sierra Tejeda Mountains. The dense forests also support a rich variety of birdlife and other local fauna.

Q: Is there parking available near the mountain bike trailheads in Alcaucín?
A: While specific parking details vary by trailhead, Alcaucín is generally accessible for visitors with vehicles. Many routes start from or pass through the village, where public parking is typically available. For routes starting further afield, local information or komoot route details often provide guidance on suitable parking spots.
tips: A permanent spring with clean mountain water cherished by locals who drive long distances to fill their containers here
Excellent spot. The restaurant is now closed permanently.

"La Alcaúca" Controlled Camping Area.

Located in the municipality of Alhama de Granada, in the Sierra de Tejeda, Almijara, and Alhama mountain ranges (Granada). Access is from the A-402 road (Alhama–Zafarraya) at km 39.8, followed by a 3 km forest track.
Recreational area with tables, barbecues, water fountains, and restrooms.
Wooded surroundings: predominantly Aleppo pines and gently sloping terraces.
Great vista point across the high valley to the Pico de Puerta, with the Castello de Zafaraya in the lower right.

Old train tracks that came from Vélez-Málaga and that used rack technology in the last section between La Viñuela, Periana and Ventas de Zafarraya. Rack trains had sprockets at the front that hooked into a third rail to navigate steep inclines.

This route is mentioned in the work "The Face of Spain" by Gerald Brenan: "Past Vélez, the train began to climb on a rack railway. All afternoon it meandered between hills of reddish earth covered with vineyards, then descended to with devilish speed, and climbed back towards the pass."

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: This challenging mountain biking route is approximately 35.8 km long and typically takes around 3 hours and 48 minutes to complete. However, your actual time may vary depending on your fitness level, technical skill, and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is rated as difficult. The Sierra de Tejeda region features rugged terrain with rocky singletrack, steep climbs, and technical descents, often requiring advanced riding skills and a full suspension bike. It's best suited for experienced mountain bikers with good fitness.

faqs.question: Where can I park when starting this route from Puente de Don Manuel?
faqs.answer: The route starts from Puente de Don Manuel. While specific parking details for this exact trailhead aren't provided, you can generally find parking within or near the village. It's advisable to arrive early, especially during peak seasons, to secure a spot.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: You can expect a diverse and challenging terrain. The route includes a mix of gravel tracks, which can sometimes be deep, rocky singletrack with 'chunky' descents, and winding forest trails. There are significant elevation changes with demanding climbs and fast, technical descents, characteristic of the mountainous Sierra de Tejeda landscape.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers spectacular scenery and several points of interest. You'll pass by the Fuente Eduardico, and enjoy panoramic views from the Miguel Álvarez Viewpoint and Azafranero Viewpoint. You'll also encounter sections of the Old Rack Railway Route (Vía Verde de la Axarquía).

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other trails. You'll ride along parts of the Andalucía - South Route (Provincia de Granada), Alcázar - La Maroma, and the [CIMA ML14] Boquete de Zafarraya, among others, offering a varied riding experience.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Sierra de Tejeda, Almijara, and Alhama Natural Park generally allows dogs, it's crucial to keep them on a leash, especially given the presence of diverse wildlife like ibex and wild boar. The challenging terrain and long duration of this specific route might also be strenuous for some dogs.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access this route in the Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Typically, there are no entrance fees or specific permits required for general mountain biking on established trails within the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park. However, always check local regulations before your visit, as rules can change, especially for specific activities or protected areas.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to mountain bike in the Sierra de Tejeda region is generally during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out.

faqs.question: What essential gear should I bring for this challenging mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and terrain, it's highly recommended to bring a full suspension mountain bike. Essential gear includes a helmet, appropriate protective pads, plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit (spare tube, pump, multi-tool), navigation device (like a GPS or komoot app), and suitable clothing for varying weather conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or places to refuel along or near the starting point?
faqs.answer: The route passes by La Alcaúca, which may offer some facilities. Additionally, Puente de Don Manuel, as a village, is likely to have cafes or small shops where you can refuel before or after your ride. It's always a good idea to carry enough supplies for the duration of your ride, as services might be limited on the trail itself.

waypoints.name: Old Rack Railway Route (Vía Verde de la Axarquía)
